rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself against prior mass-customization work focused on first-purchase utility benefits, and the cited 2005 JMR paper is exactly about utility/complexity trade-offs in mass customization configurations. Given this direct conceptual overlap (mass customization, utility mechanisms) and the citing paper’s stated contrast to that literature, the citation is plausibly foundational rather than incidental. With only DOI-level match but strong topical alignment in the notes, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
